member.name: T:FinStream.API.Controllers.InstrumentsController
member.summary: This controller handles all requests related to financial Instruments (like stocks, e.g., AAPL, GOOG). In an API, a controller's job is just to be the "receptionist". It takes the HTTP request, talks to the database (via Repositories), and returns a formatted JSON response.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.InstrumentsController.GetAll
member.summary: Gets a list of all active financial instruments in the system.
member.returns: A list of instruments (e.g. AAPL, GOOG, MSFT).

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.InstrumentsController.GetBySymbol(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the details of a specific instrument using its ticker symbol.
member.param: The ticker symbol of the instrument (e.g., AAPL).

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.InstrumentsController.Create(FinStream.Application.DTOs.CreateInstrumentDto)
member.summary: Adds a new financial instrument to the tracking system.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.InstrumentsController.Delete(System.String)
member.summary: Removes an instrument from the tracking system.
member.param: The ticker symbol of the instrument to delete.

member.name: T:FinStream.API.Controllers.MetricsController
member.summary: This controller fetches the mathematical calculations (Metrics like Moving Averages) for our financial instruments. It implements the "CQRS" pattern by reading the latest state directly from a high-speed Redis Cache instead of the slow SQL database.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.MetricsController.GetLatest(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the absolute latest calculated metrics for a given instrument.
member.returns: The most recently calculated metrics from the fast cache.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.MetricsController.GetHistory(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the historical metrics for a given instrument.
member.param: How many records to return per page

member.name: T:FinStream.API.Controllers.RulesController
member.summary: This controller manages the trading rules (e.g., "Alert me if Volatility > 5%"). Since this is the Read API, when a user creates a rule, we save it to the database and then use Redis Pub/Sub to instantly notify the background Processor app so it can reload its rule engine.

member.name: T:FinStream.API.Controllers.SignalsController
member.summary: This controller allows users to query the historical "Signals" (Alerts) that have been generated by our background Rule Engine.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.SignalsController.GetAll(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a paginated list of all signals ever triggered.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Controllers.SignalsController.GetBySymbol(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets signals specific to a single instrument.

member.name: T:FinStream.API.Program
member.summary:
Main program class for the FinStream REST API. This API is the "Read side" of our CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) architecture:
- CQRS Pattern: We separate the "write" operations (creating/updating data) from the "read" operations (querying data). This API ONLY reads data.
- What this API does: 1. Serves historical data from the SQL Database 2. Serves current state from the Redis cache (fast reads) 3. Handles WebSocket connections for real-time updates 4. Provides REST endpoints for managing instruments, rules, metrics, and signals
- What this API does NOT do: 1. It does NOT run the MarketFeedService (that generates fake market data) 2. It does NOT run the MetricsProcessorService (that calculates indicators) 3. It does NOT evaluate rules or save new metrics/signals
This separation allows us to scale the "read" side independently from the "write" side and simplifies the code by having each component do one thing well.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.Program.Main(System.String[])
member.summary: Main entry point. This method is called when the application starts. It configures all services, middleware, and endpoints.

member.name: T:FinStream.API.WebSocketExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for mapping WebSocket endpoints. These methods add WebSocket routing to the application.

member.name: M:FinStream.API.WebSocketExtensions.MapWebSocketManager(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.WebApplication)
member.summary: Maps the WebSocket endpoint at /ws/stream. Clients connect here to receive real-time market data updates.
